---
title: "Motivation 3 — MIO: exact best subset"
subtitle: "Solve the discrete problem directly"
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
*Draft outline — content to come.*
:::

## The mixed-integer formulation

$$
\min_{\beta, z}\; -\tfrac{1}{n}\,\ell(X\beta) \quad
\text{s.t.}\;\; z_j \in \{0,1\},\;\; \textstyle\sum z_j \le k,\;\; |\beta_j| \le M z_j.
$$

## What MIO gets right

- Exact solution of the original $\ell_0$ problem.
- Branch-and-bound with modern solvers (Gurobi, CPLEX, `bestsubset`).
- No relaxation bias.

## Where MIO breaks down

- **Computational cost** explodes beyond $p \gtrsim$ a few hundred.
- **Overfits** because there is no shrinkage — every selected coefficient is fully fit.
- Requires a careful Big-$M$ choice.
- Multinomial / GLM extensions are even slower.

---

[← Lasso](02-lasso.qmd) · [Next → COMBSS preview](04-combss.qmd)
